# services.minetest-server

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.minetest-server`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.minetest-server.config` | `attribute set of anything` | Settings to add to the minetest config file. This option is ignored if `configPath` is set. |
| `services.minetest-server.configPath` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the config to use. If set to null, the config of the running user will be used: `~/.minetest/minetest.conf`. |
| `services.minetest-server.enable` | `boolean` | If enabled, starts a Minetest Server. |
| `services.minetest-server.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Additional command line flags to pass to the minetest executable. |
| `services.minetest-server.gameId` | `null or string` | Id of the game to use. To list available games run `minetestserver --gameid list`. If only one game exists, this option can be null. |
| `services.minetest-server.logPath` | `null or absolute path` | Path to logfile for logging. If set to null, logging will be output to stdout which means all output will be caught by systemd. |
| `services.minetest-server.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port number to bind to. If set to null, the default 30000 will be used. |
| `services.minetest-server.world` | `null or absolute path` | Name of the world to use. To list available worlds run `minetestserver --world list`. If only one world exists, this option can be null. |
